title: 1:1 — Dani Osei & Tal Kimura, May 20 2026
compiled_truth:
Recurring 1:1 between [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura), held over video on the afternoon of May 20, 2026. Dani had requested the sync earlier that week after running into some ambiguity around how the edge deployment roadmap overlaps with Tal's work on the authentication pipeline.

The first chunk of the conversation was about staffing. Tal mentioned that the systems team is stretched thin — two people out on leave and a third ramping on a new project — and asked whether Dani's side could lend a contractor for a few weeks to help with regression testing on the single-pass primitives. Dani was open to it but flagged that their own timeline for the next milestone at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) is tight, especially with the demo prep happening in parallel. They agreed to revisit after the Wednesday standup once they had a clearer picture of availability.

A good portion of the meeting focused on a customer escallation that came in late Friday. One of the early pilot accounts reported intermittent hash mismatches in their staging environment. Tal walked through the initial triage — it looks like the issue is on the client's ingestion side, not in Anchor's pipeline, but they want to be sure before responding. Dani suggested looping in the solutions engineering team to do a joint debug session with the customer, which Tal agreed was the right call.

They also briefly touched on the upcoming architecture review scheduled for early June. Tal wants to propose some changes to how Anchor handles session state in the deterministic pipeline, arguing that the current approach introduces unnecessary latency under concurrent load. Dani pushed back slightly, noting that stability matters more than optimization at this stage, but encouraged Tal to write up a one-pager so the broader team can weigh in.

There was a quick aside about conference season — Dani mentioned they'd been asked to moderate a panel at a data infrastructure summit in September and wanted Tal's opinion on whether it made sense to accept. Tal thought it was a good visibility play for Anchor Codec and suggested preparing a short talk on deterministic pipelines as a backup in case the panel fell through. Meeting wrapped at about 35 minutes.
timeline: - **2026-05-17** | Customer escalation received from early pilot account re: hash mismatch in staging
- **2026-05-20** | 1:1 between [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura); discussed staffing, customer issue, and architecture review prep
- **2026-05-22** | Follow-up at Wednesday standup to assess contractor availability for regression testing
- **2026-05-29** | Joint debug session held with pilot customer and solutions engineering team
- **2026-06-04** | Architecture review meeting; [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) presents session state proposal
- **2026-06-17** | Next scheduled 1:1 between Dani and Tal
- **2026-09-11** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) moderates panel at data infrastructure summit
- **2026-10-02** | Tal's session state changes merged into [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) main branch after extended review
